cutt_off: WASSCE aggregate: 24 or better; SSSCE aggregate: 24 or better
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Passes (A1-C6) in English Language, Mathematics, Integrated Science plus Credits (A1-C6) in three relevant elective subjects including Physics and Elective Mathematics. Aggregate 24 or better.
SSSCE: Passes (A-D) in three core subjects plus passes (A-D) in three relevant elective subjects including Physics and Elective Mathematics.
Technical Institute graduates with credit passes in English, Maths, Integrated Science plus relevant elective courses. D7 holders may access 2-month bridging programme.
MVT II holders enrol at Level 200; MVT III holders at Level 300. HND graduates enrol at Level 300. Mature applicants: 25+ years, must pass written test.
level_note: KTU's flagship interdisciplinary engineering programme combining mechanical, electrical, and computer engineering.
HND graduates with requisite specialisation enrol at Level 200 (unique to Mechatronics).

faculty_name: Faculty of Health and Allied Sciences
note: Houses the Departments of Biomedical Engineering, Medical Laboratory Sciences, and Rehabilitation Engineering.
Offers BTech, HND, and Certificate programmes in health sciences and biomedical engineering.

fees: Morning: GHC 4,307 (Level 100), GHC 3,929 (Level 200-300), GHC 4,803 (Level 400) — 2025/2026 (highest BTech fee at KTU)
cutt_off: WASSCE aggregate: 24 or better; SSSCE aggregate: 24 or better
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Passes (A1-C6) in English Language, Mathematics, Integrated Science plus Credits (A1-C6) in three relevant science elective subjects (Biology, Chemistry, Physics). Aggregate 24 or better.
SSSCE: Passes (A-D) in three core subjects plus passes (A-D) in three relevant science elective subjects.
GCE 'O' and 'A' Level: Six GCE 'O' Level passes including English, Maths, General Science plus three 'A' Level passes in relevant science subjects.
Mature applicants: 25+ years, must pass written test in English, Mathematics and General Knowledge.
level_note: KTU's highest-fee BTech programme (GHC 4,803 at Level 400) due to intensive lab requirements.
Graduates serve in GHS hospitals, private laboratories, and research institutions.

cutt_off: WASSCE aggregate: 24 or better; SSSCE aggregate: 24 or better
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Passes (A1-C6) in English Language, Mathematics, Integrated Science plus Credits (A1-C6) in three relevant science elective subjects (Biology, Chemistry, Physics). Aggregate 24 or better.
SSSCE: Passes (A-D) in three core subjects plus passes (A-D) in three relevant science elective subjects.
GCE 'O' and 'A' Level: Six GCE 'O' Level passes including English, Maths, General Science plus three 'A' Level passes in relevant science subjects.
Mature applicants: 25+ years, must pass written test in English, Mathematics and General Knowledge.
level_note: Trains biomedical engineers for hospitals and health technology companies.
Graduates manage, maintain, and design medical equipment in healthcare facilities.

cutt_off: WASSCE aggregate: 24 or better; SSSCE aggregate: 24 or better

SSSCE: Passes (A-D) in three core subjects plus passes (A-D) in three relevant science elective subjects.

Mature applicants: 25+ years, must pass written test in English, Mathematics and General Knowledge.
level_note: Unique rehabilitation engineering programme — one of very few in West Africa.
Graduates design assistive devices and prosthetics for people with disabilities.
